ops.title: ccx exec (monty codeexec sandbox) shipped v0.4.0; gomonty embeds an ad-hoc-signed dylib that notarized macOS binaries reject at Dlopen without a disable-library-validation entitlement
ops.body:
`ccx exec` (internal/codeexec) runs a monty-subset Python script whose async host functions are every ccx op + gated `sh()` + every stateless MCP server's tools (auto-reflected from `claude mcp list`, no flag). Only the return value enters context. Shipped v0.4.0 (2026-07-03). Built on `github.com/ewhauser/gomonty` (pins pydantic/monty by git rev).

**Why (the non-obvious release gotcha):** gomonty `go:embed`s a per-platform monty dylib that is **ad-hoc (linker-)signed**. The released darwin binaries are quill-signed + notarized → hardened runtime → **library validation**, which rejects the ad-hoc dylib at `purego.Dlopen` on arm64 (the flagship platform). Fix is the `com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation` entitlement (`.github/macos/entitlements.plist` → goreleaser `notarize.macos.sign.entitlements`). This is **invisible in local `task build`** (unsigned) — only the released, signed artifact hits it, so a post-release macos smoke job (`ccx exec '40+2'`) is the only real test. gomonty ships **no darwin/amd64** lib (Intel macs): a build-tag stub (`internal/codeexec/support_stub.go`, `codeexec.Supported`) makes `ccx exec` fail fast there while every other command works — keep the target, don't drop it (dropping cascades into the shared homebrew-tap render action + plugin shim).

**How to apply:** bumping gomonty is a dedicated PR (re-run codeexec suite + the 4 episode replays; the monty subset text in `preamble.go` is version-coupled). `claude mcp list` latency floors every CLI `ccx exec` even on catalog-cache hit (~4.5s here) — the disk catalog caches schemas, not the probe. Reflected servers get a loud per-server note; `CCX_EXEC_MCP=off` / `_DENY` / `_ALLOW` override. Measured on the production binary: JSON projection 724×, full-file signature sweep 69×.

Related: [[cc-context-architecture]], [[capt-hook-cc-context-wiring]], [[jj-shared-workingcopy-collision]].
